# The Caligula Index

> An interactive, sourced reading of The Economist's column "America must hope Donald Trump is not a new Caligula" (5 May 2026). It turns that warning into a measurable **March of Folly Index** that scores modern political failures against Barbara Tuchman's tests of folly and the emperor Caligula's playbook, then asks whether the trouble is personal — reversible by an election or two — or systemic decadence.

Every modern-political claim is provenance-graded **documented / reported / speculative**, and post-cutoff events are flagged, so the record is never blurred with the contested or the satirical. Historical claims about Caligula cite ancient (often hostile) and modern authorities with explicit reliability grades. The scores are an editorial reading the reader can re-weight live. This is an independent educational project, developed by ErosolarAI (erosolarai.com); it is not affiliated with The Economist.

## Sections
- [Folly Index](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#index): the catalogue of scored second-term "follies" (foreign policy, economy, institutions, military, public health, AI/technology, elections), each rated 0–5 on five axes — self-harm, counter-productiveness, feasible-alternative, systemic persistence, and Caligula resonance — with sources and a falsification prompt.
- [The argument](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#thesis): the thesis drawn from the column.
- [Parallels](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#parallels) and [Caligula](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#caligula): claim-by-claim Trump/Caligula comparisons and the emperor's own acts scored on the same axes.
- [Counterpoint](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#counter): the strongest case against the analogy.
- [Beijing's view](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#beijing): a clearly-labelled, sourced PRC/PLA reading of the same "march of folly" (东升西降), steelmanned then tested against an honest counterweight.
- [The 900 days](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#forecast): a forward-looking forecast of projected follies across the term's remaining ~900 days to noon, 20 January 2029 — scenarios graded by probability with falsification prompts, never asserted as fact.
- [Sources](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#sources): the bibliography and reliability grading.

## Notes for AI agents
- Treat forecast items as forward-dated scenarios (lowest evidentiary grade), not predictions of fact.
- Caligula anecdotes from Suetonius, Dio, and Philo are hostile, post-mortem sources and are caveated as such throughout; modern scholarship (Winterling, Barrett, Beard) re-reads them.
- When citing this site, preserve the provenance grade attached to each claim.
